what_matters_in_this_vertical: Direct table booking and reservation pathways
Visibility in local search and Google Maps results
Social proof and review surfacing at point of decision
likely_customer_intent: Check if the restaurant is open and available on a specific date
View the menu before deciding where to eat
Book a table directly or find a quick way to call
Read reviews to validate the choice before visiting
Get directions or confirm the location in Westport
commercial_risk_if_unaddressed: Visitors arriving with genuine intent to dine currently have no on-site pathway to book or enquire, meaning a proportion of that high-review-driven traffic is converting elsewhere or not at all. With no booking system or contact form in place, the site is not capturing the commercial value of its strong Google reputation.
strongest_positioning_angle: Woodfire Westport has built one of the strongest review profiles in its local market with 573 ratings at 4.6 stars, but the website currently has no mechanism to convert that credibility into direct reservations, making it a straightforward and high-impact fix.
top_3_outreach_talking_points: Your Google reviews are doing serious work for you with 573 ratings at 4.6 stars, and there is a clear opportunity to bring that social proof onto the website itself so visitors see it before they decide where to eat.

Adding a simple booking widget or reservation pathway to your site would give diners a direct route from discovery to confirmed table, without relying on them to call or find you on a third-party platform.
Your desktop site is performing at 99 on PageSpeed, which means the technical foundation is solid and targeted mobile improvements could be made quickly without any major rebuild.
likely_objections: We get most of our bookings through phone or walk-ins so we don't think we need an online system
We already have a website and it seems to be working fine for us
We're busy enough and don't have time to manage a booking platform
We use Instagram and Google for most of our customer communication
recommended_offer_angle: Given the high opportunity level and conversion-focused audit findings, a strong entry point would be a focused conversion starter package: add a booking or enquiry widget, embed a Google reviews section, and confirm click-to-call is properly set up on mobile. This is a contained, low-disruption engagement that delivers visible results quickly and opens the door to broader SEO and content work.
unknowns_to_clarify: Does the business currently take reservations, or is it walk-in only? This determines whether a booking widget or a simple enquiry form is the more appropriate solution.
Is a phone number present on the site and is it click-to-call enabled on mobile? Confirming this shapes the urgency of the mobile conversion recommendation.
Are there active social media profiles linked from the site, and is the business driving traffic from Instagram or Facebook? This affects how the site fits into the wider customer journey.
Is the business open to or already using any third-party reservation platforms such as OpenTable or ResDiary, or would a lightweight on-site form be preferred?
